name: MediaCrawler MCP Server
what_it_does: MediaCrawler MCP server enabling AI agents to crawl and extract content from major Chinese social media platforms — Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book), Weibo, Douyin (TikTok China), Bilibili, and Zhihu — fetching posts, comments, and user data for research and content monitoring workflows.
use_cases: Monitoring Chinese social media trends from market research agents
Scraping Xiaohongshu posts and comments from brand monitoring agents
Extracting Weibo content for social listening from analytics agents
Gathering Douyin video metadata from content research agents
Searching Zhihu Q&A content from knowledge extraction agents
Competitive intelligence on Chinese platforms from business research agents
not_for: High-volume production scraping (platform rate limits and anti-bot measures)
Teams without understanding of Chinese platform ToS
Western social media platforms (use Twitter/Reddit-specific MCPs)
best_when: An agent needs to research content across Chinese social media platforms — particularly Xiaohongshu and Weibo — for market research, trend analysis, or content monitoring.
avoid_when: You need Western social media data, or require stable production scraping — platforms aggressively update anti-bot measures.

auth.notes: Platform cookies required for each social media platform. Must manually login to Xiaohongshu, Weibo, Douyin, etc. and extract cookies. Cookie-based auth is fragile and expires.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free open source; platform accounts required
pricing.paid_tiers: None for the MCP; platform accounts may have commercial use restrictions
pricing.notes: Community MCP is free. Social media accounts and platform ToS compliance required. Commercial use may violate platform policies.

performance.rate_limits: Platform-dependent; aggressive anti-bot measures on all supported platforms

known_agent_gotchas: Cookie-based auth expires frequently — requires manual re-login for each platform
Anti-bot detection is aggressive — scraping may break without warning after platform updates
Unofficial scraping — violates most platform ToS; commercial use is legally risky
Chinese platform account required for each service — phone number verification often needed
Scraping Chinese platforms from outside China may require VPN
Platform API changes break scrapers frequently — no stability guarantee
agent_readiness.security_notes: Unofficial scraping of Chinese platforms. Cookie auth stored locally. HTTPS for platform connections. Significant legal and ToS risks for commercial use. Platform accounts can be banned. Do not scrape personal user data beyond research use.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cookie auth fragile and expiry-prone. Unofficial scraping with ToS and legal risks. Store cookies securely. Account ban risk.
